markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Black Ash](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3746)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>Cast a Spell</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

On certain rare occasions, when a particularly despoiled tree or a powerful demonic fungal infestation (such as a Jeharlu Spore) is destroyed, the grimy black ash that remains behind functions as a [catalyst](/Traits.aspx?ID=791) called black ash. A [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752) empowered with this catalyst gains the [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614) trait and appears diseased and toxic, with greasy filaments of dripping fungus growing through its vines. A creature damaged by this wall’s thorns takes an additional amount of [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The persistent damage is 1d6

</title>

</traits>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Antifungal Salve](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3747)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 77

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This foul-smelling pink paste is traditionally kept in a tightly sealed jar. Spreading the salve on exposed skin grants an item bonus to saving throws against all [afflictions](/Rules.aspx?ID=2765) that have the [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614) trait or that originate from creatures with the fungus trait. The bonus lasts for 6 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Limited"
>
Greater Antifungal Salve
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 77

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +4 item bonus.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Limited"
>
Major Antifungal Salve
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 77

**Price** 675 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +4, and when you apply the salve, the target can immediately attempt a saving throw against one fungal affliction of 14th level or lower that is affecting them. If they succeed, the affliction is neutralized.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Limited"
>
Lesser Antifungal Salve
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 77

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"
>
Moderate Antifungal Salve
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 77

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus.
name: Antifungal Salve

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Anima Robe (Artifact)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3750)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight](/Sources.aspx?ID=255) pg. 72

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +33; precise [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411) and hearing within 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (partner only)

**Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +35, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +34, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +35

**Int** +6, **Wis** +5, **Cha** +7

**Will** +33 

</column>

---        

The artistic echo of the Ekujae hero Iyalirrin animates the _Anima Robe’s_ stitching to display embroidered reactions to situations experienced by the wearer. The _Anima Robe’s_ telepathic voice is filled with confidence (almost to the point of cockiness) and is supportive, often encouraging his partner to take risky or showy actions yet never to the extent that would put them in significant harm’s way.

The _Anima Robe_ grants a +3 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks made to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) and to all [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks, and you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 15 to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage.

**Activate—Billowing Distraction** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** The _Anima Robe_ billows as if in a blustery wind and attempts to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390) a creature you’re adjacent to.

**Activate—Who Am I?** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** The robe’s hood rises up over your head, casting a 4th-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) to your specifications.

**Activate—Who Are We?** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The robe’s hem swishes, casting a 7th-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) to your specifications.

**Activate—Who Are They?** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** An image of a creature shows up in the robe’s stitching, then appears to come to life; the robe casts an 8th-rank [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567) to your specifications. The robe can use an action to [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this activation for up to 1 minute.

_Nethys Note: the non-artifact version of the item is [here.](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3697) This artifact's heroic legacy, and its subsequent skills and ability attribute, are presented [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=3419)._

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Anima Robe (Artifact)

May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight](/Sources.aspx?ID=255) pg. 72 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- **Perception** +35; precise [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411) and hearing within 30 feet **Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (partner only) **Skills** [Demon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +33, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +34, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +35 **Int** +5, **Wis** +7, **Cha** +6 **Will** +35 --- The stately echo of Jininsiel, the woman who guided her people through the Darklands to escape the devastation of Earthfall, causes _Fiendbreaker_ to softly shimmer with a silvery radiance equivalent to that of candlelight. _Fiendbreaker_ can activate or deactivate this radiance as a free action once per round on her partner’s turn. _Fiendbreaker’s_ telepathic voice is calm and soothing, seeking to support her partner’s decisions with compliments or warnings against overconfidence as needed. _Fiendbreaker_ functions as a _[+3 major striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [holy ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842) [high-grade cold iron ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)_. While wielding the staff, you gain a +3 circumstance bonus to checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about any sort of [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from her list. _Fiendbreaker_ can use this activation only if you’re holding the staff and only if you haven’t used Fiendbreaker to Cast a Spell this round. Likewise, if _Fiendbreaker_ Casts a Spell from the staff, you can’t activate the staff to Cast a Spell this round.
  • **Cantrip** [_divine lance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498)
  • **1st** [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660)
  • **2nd** [see the unseen](/Feats.aspx?ID=2815)
  • **3rd** [_anointed ground_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1965), [_holy light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557)
  • **4th** [_clear mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), [_planar tether_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636)
  • **5th** [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499)
  • **6th** _holy light_, [_spirit blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685)
  • **7th** [_divine decree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495), [_planar seal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1635)
  • **8th** _divine wrath_, _holy light_
  • **9th** _banishment _, _divine decree_
**Activate—Restore Mind** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** _Fiendbreaker_ attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) an ongoing mental affect you’re suffering from, with a counteract rank of 9th and a +25 modifier to the roll (or a +30 modifier if the source of the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect on you was created by a [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600)). _Nethys Note: the non-artifact version of the item is [here.](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3698) This artifact's heroic legacy, and its subsequent skills and ability attribute, are presented [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=3419). _

May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight](/Sources.aspx?ID=255) pg. 73 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- **Perception** +33; precise [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411) and hearing within 30 feet **Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (partner only) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +35, [Architecture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +35, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +35 **Int** +7, **Wis** +5, **Cha** +6 **Will** +33 --- The stoic echo of Candlaron the Sculptor, architect of the aiudara network, infuses the _Guiding Star Orb_. As long as you have him invested, the _Guiding Star Orb_ orbits your body instead of needing to be held in one hand. You can stow the _Guiding Star Orb_ with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action, and he can be snatched out of the air with a successful [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) action against you. When stowed, the _Guiding Star Orb_ remains invested, but if he’s removed from you via Disarm, your investment ends if you don’t reclaim him by the end of your next turn. The _Guiding Star Orb’s_ voice is scholarly and precise, speaking telepathically with clearly enunciated but often complex words, and he assumes you’re educated enough to keep up with his academic dialogue. As long as you have the _Guiding Star Orb_ invested, [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effects created by you or the Guiding Star Orb aren’t affected by the Witchbole’s meddling with teleportation effects, and they can’t be [counteracted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) by the Witchbole. **Activate—Blinding Spark** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Effect** The _Guiding Star Orb_ creates a beam of bright light and fires it at a creature within 30 feet. That creature must attempt a DC 43 Fortitude save, after which that creature is temporarily immune to Blinding Spark for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is permanently blinded. **Activate—Embed Location** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Effect** By focusing, the _Guiding Star Orb_ embeds your current location within. Thereafter, anyone who touches the _Guiding Star Orb_ while casting a spell with the teleportation trait to travel to this location arrives precisely, without any inaccuracy at all. The _Guiding Star Orb_ can have up to three locations embedded at a time; if this activity is used a fourth time, the new location replaces the oldest stored one. **Activate—Momentary Aiudara** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** A shimmering magical archway appears next to you as the _Guiding Star Orb_ casts a 9th-rank teleport to your specifications. If you’re teleporting to an aiudara you’ve visited before, you and the targets appear precisely at that location. If you’re teleporting to Lotusgate in Kyonin, this effect functions as [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) if you aren’t on the same plane as Kyonin. _Nethys Note: the non-artifact version of the item is [here.](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3699) This artifact's heroic legacy, and its subsequent skills and ability attribute, are presented [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=3419). _
name: Guiding Star Orb (Artifact)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Slithermaw's Bane (Artifact)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3753)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight](/Sources.aspx?ID=255) pg. 73

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +34; precise [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411) and hearing within 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (partner only)

**Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +35, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +33, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +33

**Int** +5, **Wis** +6, **Cha** +7

**Will** +34 

</column>

---        

The heroism of Kyloss Syndar, founder of Greengold and slayer of the demonic hydra Slithermaw, imprinted this suit of armor with a gleaming, prismatic sheen that sparkles under bright light. The telepathic voice of _Slithermaw’s Bane_ is boisterous and grandiose, encouraging his partner to take up leadership roles while also providing advice to maintain loyalty and strengthen bonds of friendship. 

_Slithermaw’s Bane_ is a suit of _[+3 major resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [greater fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2789) high-grade [elven chain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2801)_ that grants his wearer poison [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 15, and the item bonus his resilient rune grants to saving throws versus poison increases by 1 to +4.

**Activate—Calistria’s Sting** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature [grapples](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) you;  **Effect** Poison wells up from the armor’s links to seep into the triggering creature’s body, causing it to suffer wracking pains as if it was being stung by thousands of angry wasps. The triggering creature takes 7d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Fortitude save); this persistent damage can’t be ended as long as the triggering creature continues to grapple you. _Slithermaw’s Bane_ can’t activate [Calistria’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=279) Sting using his actions.

**Activate—Purge Toxins** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** _Slithermaw’s Bane_ attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) a [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) [affliction](/Rules.aspx?ID=2389) affecting you, with a counteract rank of 9th and a +25 modifier to the roll (+30 modifier if the source of the disease or poison effect on you was created by a [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600)).

**Activate—Adaptation** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You alter the exterior of the armor to better adapt to the surrounding [terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2365): [aquatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2770), [arctic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2773), [desert](/Rules.aspx?ID=2776), [forest](/Rules.aspx?ID=2779), [mountain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2783), [plains](/Rules.aspx?ID=2789), sky, [swamp](/Rules.aspx?ID=2792), or [underground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2808). You [ignore difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) within the chosen [environment](/Rules.aspx?ID=590) and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [environmental hazards](/Rules.aspx?ID=2588), [natural disasters](/Rules.aspx?ID=2823), and extreme temperatures that originate from that terrain. You’re also protected from [severe and extreme heat or severe and extreme cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) (your choice when you activate this ability). This effect lasts until your next daily preparations or the next time you activate it, whichever comes first.

_Nethys Note: the non-artifact version of the item is [here.](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3700) This artifact's heroic legacy, and its subsequent skills and ability attribute, are presented [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=3419). _

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Slithermaw's Bane (Artifact)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Soulcutter (Artifact)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3754)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight](/Sources.aspx?ID=255) pg. 73

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +34; precise [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411) and hearing within 30 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (partner only)

**Skills** [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +34, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +34, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +34

**Int** +7, **Wis** +6, **Cha** +5

**Will** +34 

</column>

---        

The echo of the [Calistrian](/Deities.aspx?ID=279) witch Silisifex imbues her legendary blade with a shimmering pale-green glow equivalent to that of candlelight; _Soulcutter_ can activate or deactivate this radiance as a free action once per round on her partner’s turn. _Soulcutter’s_ telepathic voice is a sultry whisper that often infuses her observations with a flirtatious edge or a biting sense of dark humor. 

_Soulcutter_ is a _[+3 major striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [greater astral ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2832) [holy ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842) [elven curve blade](/Weapons.aspx?ID=406)_. As long as you carry _Soulcutter_, you gain her potency bonus as an item bonus to all saving throws against mental effects. This bonus increases by 2 to +5 against [possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=671) effects.

**Activate—Soothe Souls** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** _Soulcutter_ creates a pulse of restorative energy, rejuvenating those within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around you while castigating those in that area who have no place in nature. _Soulcutter_ can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this activation for up to 1 minute. Living creatures that start their turn in the area regain 2d8 Hit Points, and any [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature that starts their turn in the area takes 2d8 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save).

**Activate—Soulcutting Storm** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** _Soulcutter_ casts a 9th-rank [_weapon storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758) to your specification, but all damage caused by the spell is [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage. If used to damage a creature that’s possessing another creature, this spell does no damage to the possessed creature.

**Activate—Shame Demon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** _Soulcutter_ targets a [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573) she can see or hear within 30 feet, causing the blade’s green light to well up around the demon for a moment. The demon suffers the effects of its sin vulnerability, if it has any, and is then temporarily immune to Shame Demon for 24 hours. If _Soulcutter’s_ partner wasn’t aware of the normal effects that trigger that demon’s sin vulnerability, they learn it at this time.

_Nethys Note: the non-artifact version of the item is [here.](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3701) This artifact's heroic legacy, and its subsequent skills and ability attribute, are presented [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=3419). _

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Soulcutter (Artifact)

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Spore War **Source** [Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight](/Sources.aspx?ID=255) pg. 73 **Usage** held in 1+ hand(s) **Bulk** 1 --- **Perception** +35; precise [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411) and hearing within 30 feet **Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (partner only) **Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +33, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +35, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +35 **Int** +6, **Wis** +7, **Cha** +5 **Will** +35 --- The echo of the snowcaster Jelarial causes _Wintershot_ to become chill to the touch, yet despite feeling as cold as ice, she never causes harm or discomfort to her partner. Wintershot’s string issues a soft hissing sound, as of snow softly falling, and snowflakes drift down from the string and swiftly melt each time the bow is fired. _Wintershot’s_ voice is eager and excitable, often waxing poetic on a nearby object or creature of beauty or offering compliments to her partner. _Wintershot_ is a _[+3 major striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [greater frost ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839) [holy ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=435)_. When making a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with _Wintershot_, targets don’t gain [concealment](/Rules.aspx?ID=424) from the effects of mist or [precipitation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2816), and circumstance penalties to attacks imparted from strong winds are negated. **Activate—Auroral Shine** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** _Wintershot_ can’t use this activation. When you activate Auroral Shine, fire an arrow at a target. If you hit, instead of dealing damage with the arrow, the creature struck must attempt a DC 43 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is surrounded by shimmering lights akin to the aurora, causing it to become dazzled for 2 rounds. If the creature was [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), it becomes [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) instead. If the creature was already concealed for any other reason, it’s no longer concealed.
**Failure** As success, but the creature also takes 6d6 [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), and the lights affect the creature for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the creature also takes 6d6 persistent cold damage, and the lights affect the creature for 10 minutes. **Activate—Signal Flare** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** _Wintershot_ fires an arrow-shaped construct of ice straight upward. The arrow soars to a height of 500 feet, or until it strikes a solid surface like a ceiling. When it reaches its apex, it explodes in a brilliant burst, creating a 100-foot radius area of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) in the next 100 feet. In the night sky, this beacon can be seen clearly for miles. The beacon remains lit for up to 1 minute in a color of _Wintershot’s_ choice. Alternatively, _Wintershot_ can fire this Signal Flare like a normal arrow to attempt to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) a target her partner can see—if it hits, the arrow inflicts normal damage and attempts to counteract one darkness effect of Wintershot’s choice that affects the creature struck, with a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) rank of 9th and a +25 modifier to the roll (+30 modifier if the source of the [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569) effect was created by a [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600)). **Activate—Rimecrust** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631)) **Effect** _Wintershot_ causes the deep chill of winter to settle upon a single creature she can see or hear within 30 feet. That creature must attempt a DC 43 Fortitude save, after which point they’re temporarily immune to Rimecrust for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature’s discomfort and shivering from the sudden drop in temperature causes it to become [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of its next turn. Creatures that are [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to cold are immune to this effect.
**Failure** The creature becomes encrusted with ice. It becomes [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 minute, or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) from the crusting ice (DC 43).
**Critical Failure** The creature is slowed 1 and [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by crusting ice until it Escapes (DC 43). After it Escapes, it remains slowed 1 and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 minute from the lingering cold. _Nethys Note: the non-artifact version of the item is [here.](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3702) This artifact's heroic legacy, and its subsequent skills and ability attribute, are presented [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=3419). _
name: Wintershot (Artifact)

    level="1"
    right="Item 21"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Graymist](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3756)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down](/Sources.aspx?ID=247) pg. 83

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Graymist_ functions as a [_shadowmist cape_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2143).

Once restored, _Graymist_ gains these activations.

**Activate—Diverting Reveal** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** _Graymist_ manifests a dangerous-looking assassin, who appears to step out out of hiding at any point you can sense within 60 feet. This allows _Graymist_ to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387) using his [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) skill, but the closest creature to where the Diverting Reveal appears must attempt a DC 44 Will save or become so startled by the assassin’s sudden appearance that they become [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 3 (or stunned 6 on a critical failure). The assassin vanishes a moment after the diverting reveal, appearing to go back into hiding.

**Activate—Traceless Retreat** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** _Graymist_ casts [_mislead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1605) on yourself and up to three other targets of your choice within 30 feet. _Graymist_ [sustains](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this effect up to 1 minute, during which you cannot activate _Graymist_ and _Graymist_ cannot take any other actions.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />

---

_Red Hand’s Satchel_ is an [_alchemist’s haversack_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2305), with a main section containing an extradimensional space like that of a [_type IV spacious pouch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3032).

Once restored, the satchel gains the following activation.

**Activate—Instant Alchemy** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You name and then produce a common [alchemical item](/Rules.aspx?ID=2934) of any level up to level 20 from the satchel’s main compartment. This item has the [infused](/Traits.aspx?ID=797) trait and remains potent for 24 hours. Make a flat check with a DC equal to the alchemical item’s level. On failure, you can’t activate _Red Hand’s Satchel_ in this way until the next time you make your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575).

</column>

May contain spoilers from Curtain Call **Source** [Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down](/Sources.aspx?ID=247) pg. 83 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- _Retribution_ is a [_+3 greater striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [_keen_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2843) [_shifting_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2846) [_wounding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) [_war razor_](/Weapons.aspx?ID=307). **Activate—Vengeful Strike** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** You make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with _Retribution_ against a target within 120 feet that you can see and who damaged you within the last minute, as if they were within reach. Once _Retribution_ is restored, it becomes a _+3 major striking keen shifting wounding war razor_ and gains the following additional activations. **Activate—Cruel Cut** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Choose a target within 120 feet who you can see. That target must attempt a DC 44 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes 3d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
**Failure** As success, but one of their eyes is damaged, rendering them [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65). The target also becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1. The dazzled and drained condition are permanent until removed by [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) or a similar effect.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but 6d6 persistent bleed damage and the target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) instead of dazzled, and drained 2 instead of drained 1. If the target is ever reduced to 0 Hit Points or less while under this curse, they immediately die. **Activate** Reflect Curse; **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are about to attempt a saving throw against a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566); **Effect** If you succeed or critically succeed against the curse, it is reflected back to its source and it does not affect you; the source must then attempt a saving throw against the curse.

[White Cleome's Eye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3759)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

Delicate engravings of spiders and cleomes decorate the silver cover of this magnifying loupe. _White Cleome’s Eye_ functions as [_spectacles of inquiry_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2316), but the item bonuses granted to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks increase by 1 (so +3 while worn, and +4 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) when you activate the artifact).

Once restored, _White Cleome’s Eye_ gains the following additional activations.

**Activate—Look to the Past** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You twist _White Cleome’s Eye_ in a clockwise rotation, allowing you to peer back in time, causing it to cast a 9th-rank [_retrocognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652) spell.

**Activate—Relive the Past** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You twist _White Cleome’s Eye_ in a counter-clockwise rotation, allowing you to experience previous events that impacted an object you are touching, causing _White Cleome’s Eye_ to cast a 9th-rank [_object reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2012) spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: White Cleome's Eye
search_markdown:

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Viridian Crown](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3760)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #212: A Voice in the Blight](/Sources.aspx?ID=255) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The monarch of Kyonin imparts a sliver of their personality to the artifact when they are crowned ruler of the nation. The crown’s precise shape changes with their needs. A paranoid ruler might cause the crown to transform into a cowl, while a gregarious one could cause it to become a lofty piece that displays its beauty.

Atop Queen Telandia’s brow, the _Viridian Crown_ has assumed its current appearance: brimming with life, its blue-green vines moving and rustling on her brow. While the material that comprises the crown seems to be made of living vines, to the touch it is as resilient as dawnsilver. In the spring, buds bloom into crystalline flowers that give the crown a scintillating appearance. These delicate flower petals naturally fall over time as the season advances, and often serve as temporary tokens or gifts to citizens and dignitaries. The petals are art objects worth a few dozen gp each, but the act of selling them is considered crass by Kyonin’s people–and their fragility ensures that they crumble to dust before too long anyway. Their use as raw materials in the crafting of consumable magic is a timehonored tradition among elves.

If you are the properly anointed ruler of Kyonin, or if that ruler has given you permission to wear the crown for a time, as long as you have the _Viridian Crown_ invested, choose either Charisma or Wisdom. The _Viridian Crown_ increases the chosen modifier by 1 or increases it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. You can change which modifier is adjusted once every 24 hours as you make your daily preparations.

As long as you have the crown invested, it imparts knowledge of the [fortifying brew](/Rituals.aspx?ID=147) and [resurrect](/Rituals.aspx?ID=123) rituals. When you cast either of these (either as a primary or secondary caster) you gain a +3 item bonus to your skill check.

**Activate—Invoke the Crown** <actions string="" /> [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You cast one of the following spells from the crown at 10th-rank: [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [_detect poison_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1486), [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), or [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585).

**Activate—View Subject** <actions string="" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** You focus your thoughts, and the _Viridian Crown_ shows you an image of an elf who has accepted your rule. You must know the elf’s name or have met them in person. This functions as if the _Viridian Crown_ cast [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662) to your specifications. The target can resist the [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) effect with a DC 43 Will save if they wish.

**Activate—Elven Hope** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You draw upon the hope of the elven people, and exude these positive thoughts in a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). You and all allies in this area gain a +3 status bonus to saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this Activation for up to 1 minute.

**Activate—Garden Sanctuary** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** three times per day;   **Effect** You touch the _Viridian Crown_, then pass your hand outward, palm down. A glorious and beautiful garden blossoms around you in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). The plants comprising this garden are of a type appropriate to the terrain, but if no plants are appropriate, you can choose the type. The garden’s plants actively move and twist to oppose passage by your enemies, and they treat the terrain as greater [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). As long as you begin your turn in the garden sanctuary, it gains the minion trait and can use its two actions to cast spells at your direction. The garden sanctuary’s spells are cast at 9th-rank, and it has 3 spell slots to cast spells each day. It can cast [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1526), [_moment of renewal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1607), or [_tree of seasons_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1725), treating its center as the point of origin for these spells as needed. The garden sanctuary persists until you use this activation again.

**Destruction** The _Viridian Crown_ withers and dies if a rightfully coronated monarch pledges the crown to a [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) lord during a ritual that takes 24 hours to complete.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Viridian Crown

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 52

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Though it contains but a tiny drop of [Gorum’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=283) blood, drinking this thick, swirling potion fills the user with divine wrath and resilience. While under the effect of the potion, you gain a status bonus to physical damage rolls for 1 minute. The first time during that minute you’re reduced to 0 Hit Points but not immediately killed, you avoid being knocked out, regain the listed amount of Hit Points, and become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round, and your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition increases by 1.

</column>

markdown:
**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 52 **Ammunition** any **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) --- Embedded in this piece of ammunition is a shining sliver of a warshard. When an activated godrending ammunition hits a target, the body of the struck creature attempts to tear itself apart, causing nauseating pain. Instead of its normal damage, the ammunition deals 10d8 slashing damage. The target can attempt a DC 30 Fortitude saving throw; it takes a –2 circumstance penalty to this save if the Strike was a critical hit. **Critical Success** The foe takes the normal damage from the ammunition, instead of the 10d8 slashing damage.
**Success** The foe takes half damage.
**Failure** The foe takes full damage and is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Critical Failure** The foe takes double damage and is sickened 2. A creature reduced to 0 HP from this damage is ripped limb from limb, and it instantly dies; its gear remains. Godrending Ammunition **Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 52 **Price** 440 gp Greater Godrending Ammunition **Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 52 **Price** 3,300 gp --- The damage is 15d8, and the save DC is 38.
name: Godrending Ammunition

markdown:
**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 52 **Ammunition** any **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) --- Embedded in this piece of ammunition is a shining sliver of a warshard. When an activated godrending ammunition hits a target, the body of the struck creature attempts to tear itself apart, causing nauseating pain. Instead of its normal damage, the ammunition deals 10d8 slashing damage. The target can attempt a DC 30 Fortitude saving throw; it takes a –2 circumstance penalty to this save if the Strike was a critical hit. **Critical Success** The foe takes the normal damage from the ammunition, instead of the 10d8 slashing damage.
**Success** The foe takes half damage.
**Failure** The foe takes full damage and is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Critical Failure** The foe takes double damage and is sickened 2. A creature reduced to 0 HP from this damage is ripped limb from limb, and it instantly dies; its gear remains. Godrending Ammunition **Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 52 **Price** 440 gp Greater Godrending Ammunition **Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 52 **Price** 3,300 gp --- The damage is 15d8, and the save DC is 38.
name: Godrending Ammunition

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Screaming Pinion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3764)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 52

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Flintlock Musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=191)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ flintlock musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=191)_ is an innovation of the Platinum Wing, and possession of one by a civilian is a high crime in Andoran. The gun’s secret is the chip of a warshard used as the hammer in the striking mechanism.

**Activate—Transcendent Shot** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** The _screaming pinion_ is unloaded; **Effect** You call on the power of the warshard to load the _screaming pinion_ with a bullet made of divine fury. The next attack from the gun deals [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage, rather than its typical type, and on a critical hit, the target is subject to the weapon’s [critical specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203) effect. If the gun isn’t fired before the end of your next turn, the bullet disappears, and the gun becomes unloaded.

**Activate—Eagle’s Cry** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The musket unleashes a piercing sound wave in a 70-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388). Each creature in the area takes 8d10 [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage with a DC 30 basic Fortitude save. A creature that critically fails its save is also [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66). If the _screaming pinion_ has a [_+3 potency_ rune](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830), the DC increases to 35, and the damage increases to 10d10.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Screaming Pinion

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Twilight Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3765)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 52

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** You’re a member of the Twilight Talons.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo of a black eagle gripping a sword and arrows in its talons identifies the bearer as a member of the Twilight Talons. Agents typically keep the tattoo hidden unless they need to prove their identity to another member. The bearer gains a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks.

**Activate—Fade** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629))    **Effect** You hide your tattoo from view. The tattoo is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) for 1 day and can’t be detected by effects such as [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) and [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646). You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this effect. 

**Activate—Inscribe** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You lay your hand on a piece of text, and your tattoo makes a perfect copy of it, storing it as a ring of swirling letters surrounding the design. The tattoo can hold text equivalent to two pages of a book, a single [scroll](/Traits.aspx?ID=688), or a similar area of other surfaces, though it doesn’t replicate any magical effect or other special properties of the original words. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this effect, and when you Dismiss it, the tattoo copies the original text onto a blank writing surface you’re touching.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Twilight Tattoo

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Emperor's Peak Quartz Bracelet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3766)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 100

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Five Kings Mountains have access to this option._

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While most residents of—and travelers to—the Five Kings Mountains live underground, a significant number of people explore the regions’ awe-inspiring peaks. Among their many discoveries on Emperor’s Peak is a deposit of rainbow-colored rock crystal quartz with inherent magical properties that aid in survival, especially in the mountains. Dwarven artisans fashion chunks of this translucent quartz into fashionable bracelets. Wearing such a bracelet grants you a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) and [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368). This bonus increases to +3 when in mountainous [terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2365). If you attempt a Survival check to Subsist after 8 hours or less of exploration, you take only a –2 penalty instead of a –5 penalty.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Emperor's Peak Quartz Bracelet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 19"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Keep Stone Amulet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3767)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 101

**Price** 38,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The superior [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915)-lead alloy known as keep stone is as highly prized as it is expensive, and for good reason: keep stone combines the Hardness and durability of the finest metal with inherent magical protection. Not only does the metal itself resist magical effects, but anyone in proximity to a large amount of it also enjoys a degree of protection from divination magic. Dwarven crafters have attempted to make this extra protection more portable and more affordable in the form of _keep stone amulets_. Each amulet is unique in its exact shape, size, thickness, and decorations, but typically, a _keep stone amulet_ is roughly palm-sized and engraved with clan symbols, holy aphorisms, or a favorite line of poetry. 

While wearing a _keep stone amulet_, if you’re the target of a spell with the [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574) or [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) trait, the caster must succeed at a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) or lose the spell.

**Activate—Rebuff Magic** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You’re the target of a spell with a [spell attack roll](/Rules.aspx?ID=2292);  **Effect** The caster must roll the spell attack roll twice and take the worse result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Keep Stone Amulet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Memory Ribbon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3768)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 101

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Five Kings Mountains have access to this option._

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The time-honored tradition of weaving beautiful, embroidered glory ribbons throughout one’s hair and beard remains an important cultural practice among some dwarven clans, with the choice of colors and style of presentation representing status, achievements, and other significant aspects of someone’s position. Magical versions also exist that help enhance the wearer’s memory. These magical ribbons are especially popular when someone has been invited to serve as a toastmaster at a guild banquet or as a master of ceremonies at an important festival. Their use in final oral exams for high positions is, however, hotly debated.

**Activate—Read the Ribbon's Story** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You attempt a skill check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) but haven’t rolled yet;  **Effect** The _memory ribbon_ grants you a +2 item bonus to the triggering skill check to Recall Knowledge. Afterward, the ribbon becomes non-magical.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Memory Ribbon

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kols's Oath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3770)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 101

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Five Kings Mountains have access to this option._

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This filigree grants you, as the clan dagger’s owner, a +1 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) checks and to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393). Additionally, once per day, the filigree symbol can be activated to compel an enemy to act.

**Activate—Vow Unbreakable** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You command a creature within 30 feet to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) away from you, [drop prone](/Actions.aspx?ID=2295), or [release](/Actions.aspx?ID=2300) one item it’s holding. The creature can choose to perform that action as the first action on its next turn; if it doesn’t, it takes 4d6 mental damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Will save).

</column>

This filigree depiction of a warhammer in front of a kiteshaped shield grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks and to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394). Additionally, once per day, the filigree symbol can be activated to protect your allies.

**Activate—Protect the Clan!** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Protective energy releases in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), granting a +1 status bonus to Armor Class to all allies within the area. The bonus lasts for 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Splinter of Finality](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3772)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 124

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Note from Nethys: This item is a constituent part of the [Splinter of Finality](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=317) archetype using the [archetype artifact](/Rules.aspx?ID=1961) rules._

The sharpness of this dark metal splinter never dulls. By plunging it into your neck, you anchor your soul to the fragment, granting you protection from those that would harm it at the cost of weakening your body. You take a –1 status penalty to saving throws against effects that would give you the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), or [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) condition. At 10th level, the penalty increases to –2. However, you gain spirit [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) equal to your level and a +2 status bonus to saving throws against [spirits](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) or [haunts](/Traits.aspx?ID=622) and effects that would give you the [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) conditions.

**Destruction** All 13 _splinters of finality_ must be gathered and used to reconstruct _Silent Lenore_, which must then be destroyed by means of the [blunt the final blade](/Rituals.aspx?ID=69) ritual.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Splinter of Finality

</row>

---

This oversized [greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=376), infamous for the chilling laughter it emits whenever it takes a life, was crafted by the frost giant sorcerer Jorngarl from the _final blade_ named Toothy Morris after it was stolen in an attack on a Gray Gardener convoy. It functions as a [_+3 major striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) _greataxe_ that retains the unique properties of a [_final blade_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1094), trapping the souls of any it slays and preventing them from being returned to life by any means short of divine intervention, even a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual or similar magic.

</column>

    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Resonating Crystal Boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3774)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 149

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Kyonin have access to this option._

**Price** 475 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These supple leather boots are studded with tiny crystals.

**Activate—Chiming Steps** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to half your Speed. The crystals ring out with pleasant-sounding chimes that reverberate painfully in the ears of others. Each creature that you pass adjacent to during your Stride takes 4d8 [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Fortitude save); a creature takes this damage only once. A creature who critically fails the save is also [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Resonating Crystal Boots

markdown:
**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 149 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Kyonin have access to this option. When using a _shawl of seasons_, the season is the current season where the player is located._ **Price** 150 gp **Usage** worn **Bulk** L --- This woven shawl changes its color, material, and abilities with the seasons. In its usual state, it reflects the current season of the environment the wearer is in. The handsome embroidery grants you a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392). **Activate—Change of Seasons** 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You arrange the shawl on your shoulders to gain the benefits of a season of your choice until your next daily preparations. If the season you choose is the current season, you also gain a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws.
  • **Spring** The shawl becomes smooth silk covered in delicate flowers. You gain a +5-foot item bonus to your Speed.
  • **Summer** The shawl becomes light cotton with the hues of fresh grass. You’re protected from the effects of [severe heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820).
  • **Fall** The shawl becomes thin leather with an ombre of red and orange leaves. You treat [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352) as 10 feet shorter.
  • **Winter** The shawl becomes warm wool as white as snow. You’re protected from the effects of severe cold.
name: Shawl of Seasons

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fan Buckler](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3776)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 172

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

**Access** You’re a member of the  Lion Blades .

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When collapsed, a _fan buckler_ appears to be no more than an elegant wooden fan. Any attempts to discern that there’s more to the item require a successful [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) check against the [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) DC of the wielder.

**Activate—Unfurl Fan** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You transform the fan into a [wooden buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=17) or vice versa.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fan Buckler

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lion's Call](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3777)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 173

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** You’re a member of the  Lion Blades .

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Given only to highly trusted agents by the grand princes back when Lion Blades protected the Primogen Crown, these historic _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ authorized](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1864) [ shortswords](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398)_ allow a Lion Blade wielder to locate resources. 

**Activate—Find the Pride** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_locate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588) at 5th rank to learn the location of one of the following of your choice: the nearest Lion Blade safe house, shadow school, Lion Blade agent, Lion Blade kith, or a person other than yourself who’s in possession of a _lion’s call_.

**Activate—Echo the Call** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature locates you using another _lion’s call_;  **Effect** You immediately learn the location and appearance of the triggering creature. You can communicate [telepathically](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) with the triggering creature while you remain within 1 mile of each other for the next hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lion's Call

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lion's Pelt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3778)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 173

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41), [Chain Shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=43)
</row>

**Access** You’re a member of the  Lion Blades .

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Laurisa Tromaine ordered these as prototype uniforms. The fur-lined pauldrons bear the insignia of a lion with a blade in its mouth. The type of blade indicates which bard school one graduated from. When you disguise the armor via its [_raiment_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2791) rune, you can choose whether to conceal this symbol or not. You can also use the rune’s activation to quickly display or hide the insignia.

**Activate—Contact Ally** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) at 1st rank to a target that you know is also wearing a _lion’s pelt_ uniform. If you and the target share an insignia, the spell is heightened to 3rd rank.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Taldogis Badge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3779)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 173

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Taldor have access to this option._

**Price** 28 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This badge depicting a hunting [dog](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1675) is used by Eutropia’s supporters to indicate their allegiances. 

**Activate—Bark** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [subtle](/Traits.aspx?ID=522)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The hunting dog makes a single bark that only you and a single target of your choice within 30 feet can hear. If the target is a supporter of Eutropia, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks against them for the next minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Taldogis Badge

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Jaws of the Grogrisant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3780)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 173

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn circlet

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The teeth that fell off the [_Mantle of the Grogrisant_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1476) were given to the scholars of Houses Fahlspar, Lotheed, Nicodemius, and Zespire, who—after much heated discussion—created this regal circlet. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) and [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) skill checks and [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) checks against creatures that have the [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) trait. When you invest in the headband, you either increase your Wisdom modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate—Primal Empathy** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You gain the ability to communicate with nature as if you were a part of it. You cast [_telepathy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1719) at 6th rank, which can only be used to communicate with creatures that have the [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) trait.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Jaws of the Grogrisant

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Paws of the Grogrisant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3781)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 173

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Princess Eutropia commissioned Taldor’s Imperial College of Heralds to preserve the paws that fell off the [_Mantle of the Grogrisant_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1476). A few weeks later, the college presented this pair of boots. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks and saves against forced movement. When you invest in the boots, you either increase your Strength modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate—Grogrisant Leap** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The [Grogrisant’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1727) legendary strength and reflexes empower your movement. You Leap, doubling the vertical and horizontal distance of your [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) action. If you land adjacent to a creature, you can [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) that creature once as part of this action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Paws of the Grogrisant

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Heart Bloodstone of Arazni](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3783)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 109

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _Heart Bloodstone of Arazni_ represents protection. While holding the _Heart Bloodstone_, you can feel the gentle rhythm of a beating heart within the jar.

**Activate—Raise Jar** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You hold the jar aloft, and a nimbus of crimson energy surrounds your body. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.

**Activate—Proclaim Resilence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You worship [Arazni](/Deities.aspx?ID=326) or are favored by her; **Effect** You make a loud and clear proclamation of your resilience (such as “I will not fall to the hands of my enemies!”). You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to void damage equal to your level until the beginning of your next turn.

**Destruction** The _Heart Bloodstone_ turns to dust if a worshipper of Arazni is slain while holding the _Bloodstone_ and cursing her name. Any breathing creature within 10 feet of the jar inhales the choking dust, taking 4d6 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). On a failure, the creature also takes 4 [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) (8 persistent void damage on a critical failure).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Heart Bloodstone of Arazni

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Liver Bloodstone of Arazni](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3784)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 109

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _Liver Bloodstone of Arazni_ represents confidence. While holding the _Liver Bloodstone_, you feel more certain in your abilities.

**Activate—Embolden** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You critically succeed on an attack roll, saving throw, or skill check while in [encounter mode](/Rules.aspx?ID=2421);  **Effect** You gain [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to your level for 1 round.

**Activate—Arazni's Fervor** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You worship [Arazni](/Deities.aspx?ID=326) or are favored by her; **Effect** You hold the _Liver Bloodstone_ aloft, and the _Bloodstone_ glows with crimson light, filling you and your allies with Arazni’s wrath. The Liver Bloodstone casts [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451). For the next minute, you can use a free action at the beginning of each of your turns to increase the spell’s [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) radius by 10 feet. You can’t increase the emanation’s radius in any other way.

**Destruction** The _Liver Bloodstone_ melts into a puddle of poison if a worshipper of Arazni willingly submerges the _Bloodstone_ in a lethal contact or consumed [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), then licks the _Bloodstone_ clean. Each creature in a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) is exposed to the poison that destroyed the _Bloodstone_, as if they’d touched or consumed that poison.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Liver Bloodstone of Arazni

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Lung Bloodstone of Arazni](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3785)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 109

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _Lung Bloodstone of Arazni_ represents suffering. While you carry the _Lung Bloodstone_, you gain the ability to turn your pain into a weapon against your enemies.

**Activate—Strength through Pain** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You take damage;  **Effect** You allow the pain to resonate within you, making you stronger. You gain a +1 status bonus to your next [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or [spell attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=2292) before the end of your next turn.

**Activate—Admonish** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You worship [Arazni](/Deities.aspx?ID=326) or are favored by her; **Effect** You point your finger at a creature within 60 feet that has wronged you, and you verbally admonish them. The creature takes 1d4 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage for every level you have ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save against your class DC). On a failed save, the creature is also [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 (stunned 3 on a critical failure).

**Destruction** To destroy the _Lung Bloodstone_, a worshipper of Arazni must willingly impale themself with the _Bloodstone’s_ horned figurehead, dealing themselves 3d6 piercing damage and 3d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The _Lung Bloodstone_ then evaporates into a cloud of noxious vapors, as [_toxic cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722) (DC 28), that follows the creature that destroyed the _Bloodstone_ for its duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lung Bloodstone of Arazni

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Spleen Bloodstone of Arazni](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3786)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Claws of the Tyrant](/Sources.aspx?ID=266) pg. 110

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _Spleen Bloodstone of Arazni_ represents freedom. Holding the _Spleen Bloodstone_ makes you feel as if no bonds can contain you.

**Activate—Sure Footing** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You aren’t [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81); **Effect** You [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), treating both [difficult and greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) as normal terrain during the movement. For each additional action you spend, you can Step or Stride again with the same benefits. If you have a [climb](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) or [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350), you can instead Climb or Fly respectively.

**Activate—Break Bonds** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You worship [Arazni](/Deities.aspx?ID=326) or are favored by her; **Effect** The [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), and [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) conditions, as well as any Speed penalties affecting you, immediately end unless the effect is [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) and of 12th level or higher. You can use this ability even if your actions are restricted or otherwise decided for you (such as being confused or controlled).

**Destruction** If a worshipper of Arazni willingly gains the [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) condition or becomes [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) while holding the _Spleen Bloodstone_, the _Bloodstone_ explodes, dealing 10d6 [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) damage to all creatures in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spleen Bloodstone of Arazni

<traits>

---

This ruby-studded [dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2917) chalice can’t be harmed by any substance it holds, no matter how caustic. Furthermore, liquid placed within the _Chalice of Ozem_ never spills unless its carrier chooses to do so (using a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait).

**Activate—Iomedae's Blessing** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You hold the chalice and call out [Iomedae’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=285) name. The chalice casts [_dispelling globe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494) with a +19 modifier to its [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check.

**Destruction** If the _Chalice of Ozem_ is filled with Iomedae’s blood and [Arazni](/Deities.aspx?ID=326) drinks from it, it shatters.

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398)_ has the design of a fanged mouth carved into the handle. Whenever the blade draws blood, it glows with a dull red energy, as if empowered by the taste of blood.

**Activate—Drink Life** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _blood-drinker blade_ guides your hand to cut a foe and feeds you from their blood. Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with the blade. On a hit, the attack deals an additional 1d8 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). You also gain 1d8 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) for 1 minute.
