markdown:
**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 136 **Price** 2,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) --- This small hunk of slightly rusted iron gives off a faint reddish glow that occasionally pulses when touched. Tales of _penultimate heartbeats_ say that the metal attracts a creature’s spirit at the moment of death and channels it outward for a grisly display. Once applied to a weapon, a _penultimate heartbeat’s_ effects last for 1 hour. When you reduce a living creature to 0 Hit Points with a weapon under the effect of a _penultimate heartbeat_, it doesn’t perish immediately (though is visibly on the brink of death). You can [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379) the creature up to 10 feet as a free action, after which it dies in a dramatic fashion appropriate to the injury that killed it. Foes in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) you direct originating from the creature’s final position are sprayed with gore, taking 9d10 spirit damage from the release of the creature’s soul. An affected target attempts a Will save against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. This effect has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 from the shock.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is frightened 2. For the remainder of the _penultimate heartbeat’s_ duration, the creature can’t reduce the value of the frightened condition below 1 if it ends its turn where it can see you.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is frightened 3. For the remainder of the _penultimate heartbeat’s_ duration, the creature can’t reduce the value of the frightened condition below 2 if it ends its turn where it can see you.
name: Penultimate Heartbeat

>
[Phasing Trine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3891)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Whetstone" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=861" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 136

**Price** 1,900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _phasing trine_ is a metallic triangle embedded with a clear crystal designed in the shape of a bow and nocked arrow. Strikes made with a ranged weapon under the effect of a _phasing trine_ against a target who is [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=84) by or [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) to you (but not [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96)) pass through any non-magical barriers or walls in their way, though magical barriers stop the ammunition. These Strikes ignore all [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) and circumstance bonuses to AC from shields. The Strike’s damage can’t be reduced with a [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) reaction using a non-magical shield.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Phasing Trine

When you attempt a ranged Strike with a weapon under the effect of a _pinpoint arrowhead_, you treat any [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) the target has as one degree less (for example, treat standard cover as lesser cover, and lesser cover as no cover).

</column>

These whetstones are miniature stone replicas of weapons, such as a sword or an axe, though their function is the same regardless of their shape. When you attempt a Strike as a reaction (such as with a [Readied](/Actions.aspx?ID=2299) action or the [Reactive Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=5832) reaction) using a weapon under the effect of a reactive flash, the target must first succeed at a DC 19 Reflex save or be [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the attack.

</column>

    right="Item 6"

>
[Slayer's Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3894)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Whetstone" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=861" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 137

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dull red stone is shot through with glittering veins of black and silver. When you apply a _slayer’s stone_ to your weapon, choose [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=523), [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531), [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=547), [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551), [construct](/Traits.aspx?ID=563), [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=581), [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=587), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599), [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), [giant](/Traits.aspx?ID=616), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=655), [ooze](/Traits.aspx?ID=665), or both [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614) and [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668). A weapon under the effect of a _slayer’s stone_ deals an additional 1d6 precision damage to the creatures with the chosen trait or traits for 1 minute. It’s up to the GM’s discretion whether this benefit applies against a creature disguised as a creature with the chosen trait or traits.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Slayer's Stone
